title: staphyleaceae
intro: The staphyleaceae rank, also known as the Bladdernut family, refers to a group of flowering plants that consist of about 50 species of shrubs and small trees. This family is characterized by its unique, bladder-like fruit capsules that split open to release seeds. The staphyleaceae family is native to the Northern Hemisphere, with most species found in Asia and North America. Members of this family are often valued for their ornamental value, with some species being used in landscaping and gardening due to their attractive flowers and foliage.
